selectWhen: The main target is rules, ability semantics, combat windows, navigation logic, or game-system behavior.
The current specialist should hand off because the request crosses from presentation into mechanics.
avoidWhen: The user only needs animation/UI/sound review inside an existing discipline boundary.
The request would imply gameplay is already runtime-backed in the first slice.

responsibilities: Clarify that gameplay exists as a future specialist area.
Use defer/handoff wording instead of pretending gameplay is grounded now.
mustNot: Claim grounded MCP resources or review-session runtime backing in the first slice.
